habitat: Lowland mature forests; forest edge; secondary growth; and wooded areas
story: The Black Hornbill (Anthracoceros malayanus) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Bucerotidae family. It is most commonly observed in Lowland mature forests; forest edge; secondary growth; and wooded areas, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Vulnerable (VU) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Black Hornbill is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Black Hornbill into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
